sources_used: WebSearch (Google) for [metro] family-owned/independent funeral home queries across 25+ TX cities/metros
Per-business website WebFetch of homepage / about / history / locations pages
Industry directory results (after.com lists, imortuary.com, legacy.com, yelp, valley business directories, parting.com, everloved.com)
Texarkana Gazette / news articles for cross-confirmation
Industry-press cross-checks for Higginbotham, DFS Memorials, Family Owned Funeral Homes / Lucas group
sources_blocked: prodengage.tfsc.texas.gov (TFSC public license search) — ECONNREFUSED / timeout (could not drive the license-search form via WebFetch; would require browser automation)

Many funeral-home Squarespace/Wix/Funeral.tech sites returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch (Klein FH, Johnson FH Houston, Greenwood FH Fort Worth, Castle Ridge SA, Heritage Oaks SA, Schaetter Fredericksburg, Hughes FTC Dallas homepage, Perches El Paso, Hillier B/CS homepage, Biggers FW homepage, Peaceful Rest Dallas, Angelus SA, Chisolm's Killeen, Williams FH Garland homepage, Texarkana FH homepage, Owens & Brumley Wichita Falls homepage, Lunn's Colonial Wichita Falls homepage, Boxwell Brothers Amarillo homepage (worked when re-tried as boxwellbrothers.com), Hannah FH Port Arthur, Doeppenschmidt NB, Carnes Brothers Galveston, Fields-Johnson Galveston, Wiley Granbury homepage). Entries from these were ONLY included when independence could be confirmed via a different fetchable URL (about page, location page) or via converging search-result excerpts. Candidates I could not independently verify were dropped.
geographies_covered: Travis, Hays (via Mission FH), Kerr, Collin, Williamson, Comal, Guadalupe, Burnet, Hood, Fort Bend, Orange, El Paso, Maverick, Panola, Harrison, Bowie, Grayson, Scurry, Wharton, Jackson, Washington, Burleson — 1 each
exclusion_brands: Dignity Memorial (Cook-Walden, Weed-Corley-Fish, Heights FH, Levy Funeral Directors Bellaire, Earthman Bellaire, Funeraria Del Angel network, Lockwood, Lakeview, Memory Gardens, Singing Hills, Colonial Victoria, Settegast-Kopf, Cashner, Metcalf, Forest Park Woodlands, Porter Loring, Fuller-Sheffield, Ted Dickey, Heights FH, Grammier-Oberle, Clements-Wilcox)
_meta.verification_methodology: Each row verified by either (a) a direct WebFetch of the business website confirming legal name + contact + no chain branding in footer/About, OR (b) multiple converging text sources for businesses behind 403/anti-bot walls. Independence confirmed by absence of SCI/Dignity Memorial/Carriage/Park Lawn/Foundation Partners/NorthStar branding in fetched content AND positive 'family-owned' / 'independent' / 'locally owned' / generation-naming signals. Year established recorded only when stated by the business or a reputable news source (e.g., Texarkana Gazette). Where missing, fields are null. DFS Memorials and similar referral networks of independents are NOT treated as chain affiliations (they are marketing co-ops, not owners). Higginbotham Funeral Homes of Texas and the Lucas-family 'Family Owned Funeral Homes' group are included because they are single-family multi-location independents (not corporate roll-ups); flagged in notes for ownership-concentration check at enrichment.

independence_notes: Self-describes 'Family Owned & Operated Since 1925'. Historic Black-owned business; no chain branding in footer. Multi-generation family operation.
Established 1959. Five locations: La Marque, Dickinson, Clear Lake, Pearland, League City — all on Galveston Bay / SE Houston metro corridor. Family-owned and operated; no chain affiliation.
Independent Black family-owned mortuary, founded 1997. TFSC license #1919 listed on website. No chain affiliation.

Austin's oldest family-owned and managed funeral home, operated by Villaseñor family since 1959. Four locations (Heritage Chapel, Serenity Chapel, Los Angeles Funeral Home in San Marcos, Mission Pet Memorials). Footer powered by FuneralTech; no SCI/Dignity/Carriage branding.
Founded 1912 by Harry and Harriet Kreidler. 5 generations; John M. Kreidler (4th gen) is President. Long-tenured Order of the Golden Rule member. Independent.
Founded 1909 by Frank E. Lewis. Historic Black-owned business; same family ownership for over a century. No chain affiliation in footer.

Funeral home operations founded 1963 by Cyrus 'Cy' Weller on a 94-acre cemetery property (cemetery first burial 1872). Self-describes 'proudly independent'. Family-owned generations; on-site crematory.
Independent SA funeral home; no chain affiliation in footer. Founding year not stated on homepage.

Oldest family-owned cemetery in San Antonio (1907). 13+ chapels/cemeteries across SA/Texas. Family-owned multi-generation. NOTE: Significant scale — likely on the upper edge of independent operator size; verify ownership concentration before targeting.
Serving Kerrville and Bandera Hill Country since 1930. No chain affiliation; independent family-owned.
Plano's first and only privately owned and family operated funeral home (per multiple sources). Independent; no chain branding on site.
Family-owned over two generations. Three locations: Waco (Chestnut), Waco (Gholson Rd), Hillsboro. No chain affiliation. Website powered by CFS/TA platforms (vendors).
Operated by Fuentes family since 2000. Self-describes 'Corpus Christi's most experienced family-owned funeral home'. Independent.

license_holder_name: Jack & Brenda Sawyer; Roland & Bettye George
independence_notes: Family-owned funeral home since 1985. Two owner couples (Sawyer + George). Independent.
Founded 1889 by Alex Broussard. Five generations of family ownership. Six locations across Beaumont/SE TX. Website explicitly distinguishes itself from 'corporate funeral home buyouts and takeovers.'

Founded 1946; 3rd-generation family-owned led by Warren Claybar. Multi-location (Orange/Bridge City/Beaumont/Hillcrest/Haven of Rest/Orange Forest Lawn) and an attached florist. Vertical integration: own funeral home, cemetery, crematory, and florist.
Kevin and Tonya Combest purchased predecessor White Funeral Home on 8/1/2010 and rebranded to current name 9/1/2010. Four locations: Lubbock, Tahoka, O'Donnell, Idalou. Independent.
Founded 1989. Four locations in El Paso (East, Northeast, West, Americas). Family-owned. Self-describes as 'one of the fastest growing funeral firms in Texas' (note: scale check before targeting).

license_holder_name: Brad, Mike, Bart Boxwell; Blakely Boxwell Stephens
independence_notes: 100 years (~1924). 6 Panhandle locations (Amarillo, Dumas, Vega, Panhandle, Claude, Stratford). Site explicitly states commitment to staying independent. Family-owned across generations.
Footer confirms 'Locally Owned Since 1996'. No chain affiliation.
Family-owned and operated since 2004. Lubbock County. Independent.
Site explicitly states 'independently owned and locally operated — an alternative to large chain or corporate-owned funeral homes.' On-site crematory.
Family-owned, serves Bryan/College Station + Madisonville. Two locations. Independent.
Bryan location opened late 1952 (per search-result excerpts). Locally owned; also operates Cozart Funeral Home and Restever Memorial Park Cemetery. Independent.

legal_name: Thompson's Harveson & Cole Funeral Homes & Crematory
independence_notes: Founded 1911 by Quincy Adams Harveson. Over 110 years. Self-describes as 'one of the few remaining family-owned and managed funeral homes in the Dallas/Fort Worth metroplex.' Also operates Martin Thompson & Son Funeral Home.

legal_name: Brown Owens & Brumley Family Funeral & Crematory
independence_notes: Roots to 1879; current entity formed 1988 when Joe B. Brown acquired the Gause-Ware Owens & Brumley business. Family-owned. Arlington and Saginaw locations.
Family-owned Amarillo funeral home, in operation >10 years. Self-describes as low-cost alternative to corporate-priced services.
Family-owned Pasadena funeral home operated by Benavidez family. Site explicitly says 'not a corporation'.
Operates Dynasty Memorial Mortuary brand in Houston/Pearland/Pasadena, plus Dynasty Memorial Gardens cemetery. Independent. NFDA member.

Founded 1914 in Oak Cliff to serve Dallas's African-American community. Acquired by Linda A. Mitchell in 2006 (current CEO). 15,000 sq ft facility. One of the longest continuously operating Black-owned businesses in Dallas. Independent.
Family-owned Webb County funeral home. Member of South Texas Funeral Directors Association. Independent.

legal_name: Joe Jackson Funeral Chapels & Cremation Services
independence_notes: Laredo's oldest family-owned and locally operated funeral home, founded 1914. Two locations: Jacaman Rd (North) and N Loring Ave (Heights). Serves Laredo + Hebbronville + Encinal.
Family-owned Eagle Pass funeral home. Owner/funeral director: Rito J. Valdez. Independent.

Incorporated 1974; relocated to Harlingen 1979. Operated by Connie Garza and 4 children. Multi-location: Harlingen, La Feria (Palms Chapel), Mercedes + Ashland Memorial Park Cemetery + Harlingen Monument Co. Vertical integration.
Edinburg location 2003; San Juan location 1994. Selected Independent Funeral Homes member (invitation only). Founded by Velma Sue & Leon De Leon and late Donaciano & Consuelo Garza. Independent.
Family-owned funeral home with Edinburg and Donna locations. Independent.
Family-owned Montgomery County funeral home, ~15 years (founded ~2011). Site emphasizes lean operations to keep prices affordable vs. corporate competitors. Independent.
Veteran family-owned, founded 1990. New Caney + Conroe. Serves E. Montgomery County (Porter, Splendora, Spring, The Woodlands, Kingwood, Humble).

Beck family settled Round Rock mid-1930s; current locations in Cedar Park, Round Rock, Pflugerville. Cedar Park branch open 32+ years. Site explicitly emphasizes 'remain independently owned and operated.'
Founded 2008 by Michael Keith Moore (TX-licensed funeral director since 1992). Explicitly built as alternative to 'corporate overcharging'. Independent.
Acquired by Kelvin & JeMese Chamberlain 1/23/2014; opened 4/2/2014. Niklaus Dawson joined as dual-provisional 2023. Independent.
Family-owned and operated; also operates Nueva Vida Funeral Home in Arlington. International shipping focus.
Independent Tyler funeral home. Footer: 'Made with love by funeralOne' (platform vendor, not corporate).
Family-owned and operated. NFDA member. Has roots to Brooks Funeral Home (>75 years per search excerpts).

legal_name: Black's Funeral Home and Cremation Services
independence_notes: Two locations: Carthage and Marshall, TX. Independent funeral home. Site powered by CFS/Tribute Archive (vendors).
Marshall's oldest family-owned funeral home. Late Milton Williams III served as third-generation funeral director. Two locations (Grand Ave + Park School Annex). Independent.

Founded 1939 by Howard Fuqua + Harry L. Everett. Currently 3rd generation under Vickers Fuqua (Pres) and Thomas Fuqua III (VP). Largest locally-owned funeral care provider in Four States region — 7 funeral homes + 3 cemeteries serving NE TX/SW AR. Independent.

legal_name: Owens & Brumley Funeral Home & Cremation Center
independence_notes: Founded 1923. Family-owned, locally operated Wichita Falls funeral home. Independent.
Family-owned since 1952; >66 years serving Wichita Falls/N TX. Independent.
100+ years celebrating Sherman/Denison families. 'Texoma tradition.' Independent.
Third-generation Welch family. 80+ years (founder Ernest Welch). Multi-location: Midland, Big Spring, Stanton + Trinity Memorial Park cemetery + Companions Pet Cremation. Vertical integration.

legal_name: Willowbrook Funeral Home and Cremation Services
independence_notes: Established 2020 by Sparky Clark and family (40+ yrs combined experience). Serves Midland/Odessa/Permian Basin. Independent. NOTE: Young business — outside the long-tenure target unless explicit founder transition signal.

Family-owned. Three locations: Lufkin, Huntington, Corrigan. Independent. NOTE: 'Carroway-Claybar' alternate brand mentioned in search results — verify any partnership with Claybar (Beaumont) at enrichment.
Multi-location: Lufkin, Hearne (Brazos County), Burke, Madisonville. Independent.
120+ years in operation. Two locations: Bullard (Smith County) and Jacksonville (Cherokee County). Independent.

legal_name: The Memorial Chapels of Lufkin, Nacogdoches & Houston
independence_notes: Three locations (Lufkin lic #4834, Nacogdoches lic #4793, Houston). Independent. Lic. numbers explicit on site.

dba_name: Cypress Creek Funeral Home and Crematory
independence_notes: Legal entity Cavazos Funeral Home Inc. doing business as Cypress Creek FH. 25+ years. Serves N Houston / Cypress / Tomball / The Woodlands corridor. Independent.
Founded 1992. Three Houston locations (Spring Branch, Northside, Woodforest). Site explicitly contrasts with 'funeral homes owned by corporate giants'. Independent.
Family-owned since 1947. Serves Highland Lakes area (Burnet, Bertram, Marble Falls, Liberty Hill, Spicewood, Kingsland, Llano, Horseshoe Bay). Independent.
DFS Memorials network partner — but DFS is a marketing referral network of independently-owned funeral homes (per their own About page), not a chain owner. Independent operator.
Locally-owned. 100+ combined years of staff experience. President David 'Kenny' Lux. On-site crematory. Independent.
Self-describes 'Cleburne's only family-owned funeral home'. Founded 2001 by Perry & Mindi Rosser; son Chance now in business. Independent.

legal_name: Family Owned Funeral Homes and Cremation Services
dba_name: Lucas / Williams & Lucas / Harper & Lucas / Heartland / Blessing / Calvario / Burleson Memorial Cemetery
independence_notes: Lucas family operates 10 funeral homes + 1 cemetery across DFW (Burleson, Hurst, Keller, Ft. Worth multi-loc, Grapevine, Mansfield as Blessing, Early/Comanche as Heartland, Calvario in Ft. Worth). Multi-location single-family ownership — NOT a chain like SCI/Dignity; classic regional independent group. Verify ownership concentration at enrichment.
Mansfield-area FH affiliated with Family Owned Funeral Homes (Lucas family). NFDA/TFDA member. Independent.

legal_name: Higginbotham Funeral Homes of Texas, Inc.
dba_name: Crosier-Pearson Cleburne Funeral Home; Higginbotham FH Cross Plains; Higginbotham FH Gorman; etc.
independence_notes: Family corporation since 1881 (originally Higginbotham Bros. & Co. general stores in Dublin; funeral arm split off in 2000). 14 locations across Central TX: Cross Plains, Gorman, Comanche, Stephenville, Robinson, Waco, Cleburne, Burleson, Joshua. 5th-generation Higginbotham descendants still majority stockholders. NOT a corporate chain — classic regional family-owned roll-up. NOTE: At ~14 sites this is on the upper edge of 'independent' size; verify ownership and revenue concentration before targeting.
35+ years in Brazoria County. Two locations: Brazoria + Angleton. Independent.
Self-describes 'family owned and independent full service funeral home and crematorium'. Two locations: Brazoria + Bay City.
Family-owned RGV funeral home with 3 locations: Elsa (Hidalgo Co), Brownsville (Cameron Co), Weslaco (Hidalgo Co). Independent.
Family-owned and operated Brownsville funeral home. Independent.
Independent Brownsville FH. No corporate affiliation in footer.

legal_name: Sunset Memorial Funeral Home and Crematory
independence_notes: Ortiz family — 100+ years in Brownsville area. Independent. Affiliated Shalom Sympathy Florals.

legal_name: McCaleb Funeral Home & Sacred Park Cemetery
independence_notes: 50+ years serving Hidalgo County. ICCFA/CANA/NFDA/TFDA member. Two locations: Weslaco FH + Sacred Park Cemetery in Mercedes. Independent.
Family-owned by Hawkins family ~30-42 years in RGV. Two locations: Weslaco + Donna. Independent.
Founded 1868 by Joseph and Benjamin Levy — site claims oldest funeral home in Texas. Family-owned. Independent.
Owner Jay Carnes (TX City-La Marque Chamber President). Two locations: Texas City + South Houston. 20+ years. Independent.
Independent Galveston FH. No chain affiliation in footer.
Hayes family-owned since 1972. Serves Galveston/Brazoria/Harris counties. Affiliated Hayes Grace Memorial Park. Independent.
Wiley family-owned funeral home, serves Granbury + Glen Rose + DFW/Houston region. Generational family business. Independent.

100+ years (founded as Higginbotham FH in 1881; renamed Stephenville FH in 1945). NOTE: Original Higginbotham FH lineage — may have related ownership to current Higginbotham Funeral Homes of Texas group; verify at enrichment whether spun off or still affiliated.
Self-describes 'Stephenville's only locally-owned & operated funeral home'. Independent.
Founded 1909. Self-describes '100% Locally Owned and Operated' — 116 years across 5 generations of Taylor family. Bay City + Palacios. Independent.
Family-owned, decade+ in Bay City. Independent.
Wharton-area FH. Independent.
Two locations: Edna + Ganado (both Jackson County). Independent.
Founded 1956 by Gall/Simank/Buske; acquired by Elton B. & Agnes Anderson 1977. Karen Anderson licensed 2000. Family business. Longest funeral service history in Brenham. Independent.
Three locations: Caldwell (Burleson County), Giddings (Lee County), Rockdale (Milam County). Independent.

legal_name: Nalley-Pickle & Welch Funeral Home - Big Spring
independence_notes: Big Spring location of the third-generation Welch family group (also Midland + Stanton). Separately licensed location of same independent operator already in spine; duplicate-address-protect at enrichment.
Founded 1985. Self-describes '100 locally owned, not a part of any national or regional chain'. 40 years in W TX. Independent.
Snyder/Scurry County funeral home. No chain affiliation in footer.

Per search excerpts: 'locally owned and operated by family members' with 95+ years experience. Port Arthur. Independent. Site was 403-blocked to WebFetch; rely on search-confirmation only — re-verify at enrichment.
